title: Multiply whole numbers by 10
topic_path: Module 1: Place value, rounding, and algorithms for addition and subtraction
Topic A: Place value of multi-digit whole numbers

hints:
When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$17$| |$\swarrow\blueD{1}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{7}$  
$10 \times 17$ |$?$ |$?$ | $?$

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$17$| |$\swarrow\blueD{1}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{7}$  
$10 \times 17$ |$\blueD1$ |$\greenD7$ | $0$
$10 \times \blueD1\greenD7 = \blueD1\green70$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$30$| |$\swarrow\blueD{3}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$  
$10 \times 30$ |$?$ |$?$ | $?$

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$30$| |$\swarrow\blueD{3}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$  
$10 \times 30$ |$\blueD3$ |$\greenD0$ | $0$
$ \blueD3\green00=10 \times \blueD3\greenD0 $

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$89$| |$\swarrow\blueD{8}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{9}$  
$89 \times 10$ |$?$ |$?$ | $?$

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$89$| |$\swarrow\blueD{8}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{9}$  
$89 \times 10$ |$\blueD8$ |$\greenD9$ | $0$
$\blueD8\greenD9 \times 10  = \blueD8\green90$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$296$| |$\swarrow\blueD{2}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{9}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{6}$  
$296 \times 10$ | $?$ | $?$ |$?$ | $?$

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$296$| |$\swarrow\blueD{2}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{9}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{6}$
$296 \times 10$ | $\blueD{2}$ | $\greenD{9}$ |$\purpleD{6}$ | $0$
$\blueD2{,}\greenD9\purpleD60=\blueD2\greenD9\purpleD6\times 10$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$300$| |$\swarrow\blueD{3}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{0}$  
$10 \times 300$ | $?$ | $?$ |$?$ | $?$

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$300$| |$\swarrow\blueD{3}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{0}$
$10 \times 300$ | $\blueD{3}$ | $\greenD{0}$ |$\purpleD{0}$ | $0$
$10 \times\blueD3\greenD0\purpleD0=\blueD3{,}\greenD0\purpleD00$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:

$450 \times 10$ | $?$ | $?$ |$?$ | $?$

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$450$| |$\swarrow\blueD{4}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{5}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{0}$
$450 \times 10$ | $\blueD{4}$ | $\greenD{5}$ |$\purpleD{0}$ | $0$
$\blueD4{,}\greenD5\purpleD00=\blueD4\greenD5\purpleD0\times 10$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$2{,}700$| |$\swarrow\maroonD{2}$ | $\swarrow\blueD{7}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{0}$  
$10 \times 2{,}700$ | $?$ | $?$ |$?$ | $?$ | $?$

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$2{,}700$| |$\swarrow\maroonD{2}$ | $\swarrow\blueD{7}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{0}$  
$10 \times 2{,}700$ | $\maroonD{2}$ | $\blueD{7}$ |$\greenD{0}$ | $\purpleD{0}$ | $0$
$\maroonD2\blueD7{,}\greenD0\purpleD00 = 10 \times \maroonD2{,}\blueD7\greenD0\purpleD0$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:

$10 \times 3{,}140$ | $\maroonD{3}$ | $\blueD{1}$ |$\greenD{4}$ | $\purpleD{0}$ | $0$
$10 \times \maroonD3{,}\blueD1\greenD4\purpleD0=\maroonD3\blueD1{,}\greenD4\purpleD00$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones

$4{,}630 \times 10$ | $\maroonD{4}$ | $\blueD{6}$ |$\greenD{3}$ | $\purpleD{0}$ | $0$
$\maroonD4\blueD6{,}\greenD3\purpleD00 = \maroonD4{,}\blueD6\greenD3\purpleD0 \times 10$

When multiplying by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
